summary:
At the beginning of puberty, GnRH stimulates the release of LH and FSH which are essential for subsequent stages; hence, GnRH acts as a critical permissive factor during this period. \boxed{C}

The summary accurately captures that GnRH triggers the release of other important reproductive hormones like LH and FSH needed for puberty while keeping within two concise statements focused on clinically significant aspects related to the initiation phase of puberty. Agent2’s primary focus was correctly identifying how GnRH sets off these vital processes through its stimulatory effect rather than detailing all potential secondary effects or mechanisms involved beyond those directly pertinent to answering "which" specific hormone serves primarily underlined roles here initially setting up conditions conducive towards further developmental steps via direct stimulation actions alone without unnecessary elaboration outside core requirements specified originally posed regarding minimal content coverage needs met efficiently yet comprehensively enough still adhering strictly only what matters most contextually speaking concerning such questions asked specifically about single pivotal elements driving overall physiological transitions occurring early into human growth milestones particularly noted ones marking transition phases between childhood adolescence adulthood etc...
